title: Astephananthes bilobata
intro: Astephananthes bilobata, also known as two-lobed Astephananthes, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Lamiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and pain, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Astephananthes bilobata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Astephananthes bilobata is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in topical creams and salves to alleviate skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Astephananthes bilobata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Astephananthes. The plant is native to the southwestern United States. Astephananthes bilobata is further classified into the tribe and subfamily Heliantheae within Asteraceae. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the aster family.
